message:
style: apply clippy lint fixes and lifetime elision improvements

Addresses several Clippy lints for cleaner, more idiomatic Rust:

console.rs:
- Use map_or(default, f) instead of map(f).unwrap_or(default) for
  buffer_len in Debug impl. This is slightly more efficient and clearer.

logging.rs (tracing feature):
- Refine imports to be explicit rather than wildcard
- Use backticks in doc comments for type names (RichLogger → `RichLogger`)
- Change map_tracing_level to take TracingLevel by value (Copy type)
  instead of by reference, and dereference in caller
- This avoids the redundant ref/deref pattern

markdown.rs, syntax.rs:
- Use anonymous lifetime '_ instead of explicit 'a in pad_segments_to_width
- When the lifetime is only used for input/output correlation and not
  named elsewhere, '_ is preferred for conciseness

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -375,10 +375,7 @@ impl std::fmt::Debug for Console {
             .field("height", &self.height)
             .field("safe_box", &self.safe_box)
             .field("file", &"<dyn Write>")
-            .field(
-                "buffer_len",
-                &self.buffer.lock().map(|b| b.len()).unwrap_or(0),
-            )
+            .field("buffer_len", &self.buffer.lock().map_or(0, |b| b.len()))
             .field("is_terminal", &self.is_terminal)
             .field("detected_color_system", &self.detected_color_system)
             .finish_non_exhaustive()
